svg.text: Experimental: WAV from B&K 6-ch logger (one file per array position) · Simulation: CSV from synthetic monopole generator
Two independent recordings loaded into separate data containers — arrays recorded at different times due to channel-count limitation of
Novel protocol — enables 8-mic distributed array validation with 4-ch-per-recording hardware
Independent parallel processing — no cross-array CSM is formed at any stage

element-wise to freq-domain snapshots · Independent enable flag

Ṽ = ρ₀c₀·V — ensures dimensional consistency · Assemble x = [P₀,
Frequency-dependent resolution: Δx = λ / pts_per_λ · Fixed physical margin around source distance · Grid centred on system geometric
R(f) = (1/K)·Σ x·xᴴ across K snapshots per freq bin
Standard — applied to augmented AVS vector
Pressure: e^(−jkr)/r · Velocity: encodes range + bearing via
Wu et al. (2010) — near-field manifold; applied here to MEMS
w = R⁻¹d/(dᴴR⁻¹d) · B(r_l) = Σ_f |wᴴRw| over freq bins → dB
Power-domain combination — arbitrary inter-recording phase offset never enters calculation
Per array: normalise to unit mean power → multiply across arrays · Operates in power domain — valid for sequential recordings where phase
Novel application — product method with independent MEMS AVS arrays for near-field range resolution
2D parabolic interpolation at discrete peak · Independent x/y axis fitting · Offset clamped to ±0.5 grid cells · Used for both per-array
bearing extraction and final position estimate
Per array: refined peak → bearing ray from array centre · Solve [d₁,−d₂]·[t₁;t₂] = p₂−p₁ → intersection = source estimate · Fallback:
global peak if rays parallel or intersection off-grid
Triangulation principle — novel application to finite-difference MEMS AVS for range estimation
